mcqs.question: What is inheritance in Python Object Oriented Programming?

options.text: A way to hide data within a class
A mechanism where a new class derives properties and methods from an existing class
A technique to wrap data and methods together
A process of creating multiple objects of a class

mcqs.explanation: Inheritance allows a class to inherit attributes and methods from another class, promoting code reusability.

mcqs.question: What does the super() function do in Python?

options.text: It creates a new instance of a class
It calls the __init__ method of the parent class
It deletes the current class object
It returns the name of the parent class

mcqs.explanation: super() allows you to call methods of the parent class, commonly used to invoke their __init__ methods.

mcqs.question: Which type of inheritance is demonstrated in the following code? class A: pass class B: pass class C(A, B): pass

options.text: Multilevel inheritance
Hierarchical inheritance
Multiple inheritance
Single inheritance

mcqs.explanation: Multiple inheritance occurs when a class inherits from more than one parent class.

mcqs.question: In multilevel inheritance, what is the relationship between the classes?

options.text: Two parent classes inherit from one child class
A parent class is inherited by a child, which is then inherited by another class
Multiple classes inherit from a single parent class
Classes inherit from each other in a cyclic manner

mcqs.explanation: Multilevel inheritance creates a chain of inheritance where a derived class becomes a parent for another class.

mcqs.question: What is hierarchical inheritance?

options.text: When one class inherits from multiple classes
When one parent class has multiple child classes
When inheritance forms a complex diamond pattern
When classes inherit properties from grandparent only

mcqs.explanation: Hierarchical inheritance occurs when multiple derived classes inherit from a single base class.

mcqs.question: What is Method Resolution Order (MRO) in Python?

options.text: A way to delete methods from a class
The order in which base classes are searched when executing a method
A method to resolve conflicts between two classes
The sequence of constructor calls in inheritance

mcqs.explanation: MRO determines the order in which Python looks for a method in a hierarchy of classes, especially in multiple inheritance.

mcqs.question: What is hybrid inheritance in Python?

options.text: Combination of single and multiple inheritance only
Combination of two or more types of inheritance
Only multilevel and hierarchical inheritance
Inheritance with no parent class

mcqs.explanation: Hybrid inheritance combines two or more types of inheritance such as multiple, multilevel, hierarchical, or single inheritance.

mcqs.question: Which module in Python is used to define Abstract Base Classes (ABC)?

options.text: abc module using ABC class and abstractmethod decorator
object module using Abstract class
base module using Abstract base decorator
inherit module using abstract keyword

mcqs.explanation: Python's abc module provides ABC class and abstractmethod decorator to define abstract base classes that cannot be instantiated directly.
